raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, American Express issued a {$400.00} XXXX XXXX XXXX statement credit to my account. On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXmore than XXXX days laterthey reversed the credit with no advance notice or explanation until the same day the reversal appeared as a charge on my account. 

The only justification I was later given through customer support was that the credit had been duplicated in error. However, I was not informed of this when the credit was issued on XX/XX/XXXX, nor was I given the opportunity to dispute the charge before the reversal was applied. 

I believe this is a clear violation of the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ), which requires that consumers be notified of billing errors within 60 days and be given the chance to dispute any charges before an adjustment is made. 

In chat with customer support, I was repeatedly dismissed, the supervisor said When you saw XXXX credits instead of XXXX didn't you feel it was incorrect? Just to verify did you reached out to us like you did when you saw the reversal and the supervisor confirmed they could not comment on the legality of the issue. I was not provided with proper documentation showing the error or any explanation of why the XXXX window for notice was ignored. 

This is not simply a customer service issueit is a compliance and consumer protection issue, and Im seeking a formal investigation and appropriate resolution.
